content: Comparative analysis of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven RL reveals that the performance gap between these engineered curiosity frameworks and general 'curious agents' stems from unmodeled working memory dynamics that active learning SOTA 2024-2025 work has only begun to unpack. Just as the vague category of 'curious agents' lacks the formalized intrinsic reward structure of ICM/RND, informal definitions of 'agent' as a category mask critical differences in memory-guided curiosity that drive downstream task performance.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
